notes: DOCUMENTED TARGET — not yet recordable. driver_capability: gap:slash-command (see assessment.json): opencode's /compact (and /summarize) are in-TUI slash commands; the headless `opencode run` driver (drive-opencode-interactive.sh, steps send|wait_turn|sleep only) cannot fire them — headless opencode stores slash text as literal user content. Recording needs a live-REPL opencode driver. opencode is the lone ProcessOwnedStore adapter — the daemon reads session state from SQLite, so a /compact turn (in-place, same session) WOULD surface as a ready→working→ready arc; irrlicht's CompactionState field is dead code, so this spec asserts only the visible state arc: a priming turn, then a compaction working→ready arc, then a post-compaction turn proving the session continues. The compaction-vs-normal-turn distinction is not observable today.

text: Session appears in ready after the opencode REPL launches
First user prompt populates context: ready → working
First turn completes back to ready

After /compact (or /summarize) runs, the session settles back to ready on the SAME session (opencode compacts in place — no new session). State may briefly enter working during the summarizer call; only the post-compaction settle to ready is asserted. Must not stick in waiting.
Post-compaction user prompt drives ready → working — proves the session continues normally after /compact
Post-compaction turn completes back to ready
